question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here's the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` for a Kubernetes charm:

## Required Fields

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `type` | Must be `"charm"` |
| `name` | Charm identifier (lowercase, hyphens) |
| `summary` | One-line description (max 78 chars) |
| `description` | Full explanation |
| `base` | OS base, e.g. `ubuntu@24.04` |
| `containers` | Sidecar container definitions (K8s-specific) |
| `resources` | Must include an `oci-image` per container |

## Complete Example

```yaml
type: charm
name: my-app
title: My App
summary: A Kubernetes charm for my-app.
description: |
  Deploys and manages my-app on Kubernetes.

base: ubuntu@24.04

charm-user: sudoer  # recommended: root, sudoer, or non-root

assumes:
  - juju >= 3.5
  - k8s-api

containers:
  app:
    resource: app-image
    uid: 1000
    gid: 1000
    mounts:
      - storage: data
        location: /var/lib/app

resources:
  app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for the application

storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    location: /var/lib/app

config:
  options:
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: info
      description: Log verbosity level (debug, info, warning, error).

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: app-peers
    optional: true

provides:
  api:
    interface: http
    optional: false

requires:
  database:
    interface: postgresql_client
    optional: true
    limit: 1
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    optional: true
    limit: 1

charm-libs:
  - lib: postgresql.postgres_client
    version: "1"

links:
  documentation: https://example.com/docs
  source: https://github.com/example/my-app-operator
  issues: https://github.com/example/my-app-operator/issues
```

## Key Points

- **`containers`** + **`resources`** are the defining K8s-specific fields — each container needs a matching `oci-image` resource.
- **`charm-user`**: use `non-root` or `sudoer` rather than `root` for better security posture.
- **`assumes: k8s-api`** ensures the charm can only deploy on a K8s cloud.
- Always include `optional:` on all relation definitions (per the project conventions in `CLAUDE.md`).
- `mounts` in containers reference named `storage` entries — this is how persistent volumes are attached.
